provenStructure.why: Positions FlyFX as a tool the forwarder can use, not a vendor selling to them

workingAngles.why: Positions FlyFX as making the forwarder look good to their clients
workingAngles.usage: We often find forwarders a better rate than their usual charter contact, simply because we have a wider operator network.

workingAngles.why: Partnership language — not buy from me but let's both profit

workingAngles.angle: We work exclusively with freight forwarders
workingAngles.why: Removes the #1 fear — broker stealing the client
Demonstrates market knowledge, shows you read the same trade press
workingAngles.usage: Always reference a specific development, not a general statement

failingAngles.angle: Direct sales pitch — 'would you like to use us?'
Vague intro — 'Hi, I'm from an air charter company'

differentiatorRanking.impact: Technical moat — gets attention from specialists

currentlyWorking: Hormuz closure → energy logistics demand (energy forwarders respond to oil price references)
Gulf food imports → perishable charter demand (NL and Spain perishable forwarders)
Airline route cancellations → belly cargo loss (general forwarders respond to specific route names)
